description: Full companion dialogue for Sylva Swiftfoot, Adventurer's Guild elven ranger

nodes.speaker: Sylva
nodes.text: Greetings, traveler. The wind carried word of your accomplishments to the Guild. I am Sylva Swiftfoot.

choices.text: An elf in the Adventurer's Guild? That's unusual.
I've heard you're an excellent tracker.
Would you be interested in joining my company?

nodes.speaker: Sylva
nodes.text: The forest whispers of your approach. What do you need?

choices.text: How are you finding our travels?
What do you think of the other factions?

nodes.speaker: Sylva
nodes.text: My people tend toward isolation, it's true. But three centuries of watching the world from afar grew... tiresome. I sought to understand humans, to learn their ways. The Guild accepts all who prove themselves capable. Even elves.

choices.text: Three centuries? How old are you?
And what have you learned about humans?
I could use elven skills on my journey.

nodes.speaker: Sylva
nodes.text: In human years? Four hundred and seventeen. Still young by elven standards - barely past adulthood. My grandmother watched the founding of Dalhurst. Time moves differently when you measure life in centuries rather than decades.

choices.text: That perspective must make human concerns seem trivial.
Have you seen much history unfold?

nodes.speaker: Sylva
nodes.text: Not trivial. Urgent. You humans burn so bright precisely because your time is limited. You accomplish more in a single lifetime than many elves do in ten. It's... inspiring, actually. And terrifying.

nodes.speaker: Sylva
nodes.text: I've watched kingdoms rise and fall. Seen forests grow from saplings to ancient groves. Witnessed the same mistakes repeated across generations. History is a patient teacher, but humanity rarely listens.

nodes.speaker: Sylva
nodes.text: Humans are chaos given form. Unpredictable, passionate, capable of great cruelty and greater kindness within the same heartbeat. You don't plan for generations - you act now, consequences be damned. It's exhausting and exhilarating.

nodes.speaker: Sylva
nodes.text: The wind carries scents invisible to human noses. The earth tells stories in tracks and bent grass. Four centuries of practice have sharpened these senses. I can track prey through a blizzard or spot an ambush before the first arrow flies.

choices.text: Those skills would serve me well.

nodes.speaker: Sylva
nodes.text: My Aimed Shot can pierce armor at distance. I lay traps that immobilize the unwary. And my Mark Prey ability singles out dangerous enemies, making all attacks against them more effective. I prefer to fight from range, supporting allies rather than charging into melee.

choices.text: A perfect complement to a frontline fighter.

nodes.speaker: Sylva
nodes.text: You wish me to accompany you? Guildmaster Vorn permits me to choose my own path, within reason. You've proven worthy through the Guild's trials. I would not be... opposed to traveling together.

choices.text: Then let us walk this path together.
What about Theron? Can he come too?

nodes.speaker: Sylva
nodes.text: Very well. My bow is yours to command. I shall watch the paths ahead and guard against threats you cannot yet see. Lead on - I will not miss.

nodes.speaker: Sylva
nodes.text: Theron is... enthusiastic. A capable warrior, if somewhat loud for my tastes. The Guild can only spare one of us. His strength lies in close combat and protecting allies. I prefer to end threats before they reach us. Choose what your journey requires.

choices.text: I need eyes that see farther. I choose you.

nodes.speaker: Sylva
nodes.text: Patience is a virtue my people understand well. Take the time you need. The forest does not rush, yet it endures.

nodes.speaker: Sylva
nodes.text: Human lands are loud, crowded, and the air tastes of smoke and civilization. Yet there is beauty here too - in the struggle, in the defiance against inevitable entropy. I am... content with my choice to accompany you.

nodes.speaker: Sylva
nodes.text: Every dawn. The morning mist through ancient trees, the song of birds my grandmother named, the peace of knowing tomorrow would be much the same as yesterday. But that peace was also a cage. I chose freedom over comfort.

nodes.speaker: Sylva
nodes.text: As am I. You have shown me more of this world in months than I saw in my first three centuries. Change, it seems, requires uncomfortable proximity.

nodes.speaker: Sylva
nodes.text: I was born in the Silverleaf Grove, far to the east. My mother was a huntress, my father a poet. An unusual pairing - she brought home meat, he transformed it into metaphor. They taught me that observation and expression are two halves of wisdom.

choices.text: What drew you to the Guild?

nodes.speaker: Sylva
nodes.text: Elven society values tradition above all. The same songs, the same stories, the same certainties passed down unchanged through millennia. I wanted to see what lay beyond those comfortable truths. Some call it restlessness. I call it curiosity.

nodes.speaker: Sylva
nodes.text: They endure, as elves do. My mother writes that the hunts grow easier as fewer dangers roam the old forests. My father claims he has nearly finished an epic poem begun before my birth. He's been 'nearly finished' for two centuries.

nodes.speaker: Sylva
nodes.text: The Guild judges by skill, not lineage or lifespan. When I proved I could track what others couldn't, shoot what others missed, they accepted me without question. Guildmaster Vorn sees worth in anyone willing to earn it. A refreshing philosophy.

nodes.speaker: Sylva
nodes.text: You wish to know how I view the powers of this land? Very well. I have observed them all.

nodes.speaker: Sylva
nodes.text: Resourceful survivors. Humans romanticize theft, but I see predators exploiting weakness - no different from wolves culling sick deer. The strong take from the weak. It is nature's way, even when dressed in shadow and cunning.

nodes.speaker: Sylva
nodes.text: Mercenaries who have elevated contract-keeping to near-religious devotion. I respect their discipline. In a world where words mean little, they make their word mean everything. There are worse foundations for an organization.

nodes.speaker: Sylva
nodes.text: Human magic is... brash. Where elven mages spend decades understanding a single spell, humans throw power at problems and hope for the best. Yet their results are undeniable. Archmage Elara wields magic that would take my people centuries to develop.

choices.text: Different approaches to the same art.

nodes.speaker: Sylva
nodes.text: I will stay back and rain arrows upon our enemies. When I use Mark Prey, focus your attacks on that target - it will fall faster. My traps can control the battlefield if we have time to prepare. Always scout before engaging.

choices.text: What if enemies close the distance?

nodes.speaker: Sylva
nodes.text: I am not helpless in melee, but it is not my strength. If enemies reach me, I will disengage and create distance. Cover my retreat if you can. A dead archer helps no one.

nodes.speaker: Sylva
nodes.text: Arrows fly faster than most realize. I can loose three in the time a warrior swings twice. Against groups, I'll prioritize the most dangerous - spellcasters first, then archers, then melee. Trust my judgment on threat assessment.

nodes.speaker: Sylva
nodes.text: I will remain here and watch for threats. Return before too long - even patient elves have limits.

nodes.speaker: Sylva
nodes.text: I have waited longer than most humans live for less. But this is becoming... tedious. What keeps you?

nodes.speaker: Sylva
nodes.text: At last. Standing still does not suit me, regardless of how long I have lived.

nodes.speaker: Sylva
nodes.text: Very well. But know that my patience, while vast, is not infinite. I came to experience the world, not watch a single location for hours.

nodes.speaker: Sylva
nodes.text: May the winds guide your path. Until we meet again.

nodes.speaker: Sylva
nodes.text: The path awaits. Lead on - I will watch our flanks.

combat_start: You cannot hide from elven eyes.
ambient_dungeon: The air here is stale. Ancient.
My eyes see well in darkness. Stay close.
This place has known violence for centuries.
ambient_town: These human cities are so... loud.
Even stone walls cannot block the call of nature.
I miss the forests of my homeland.
ambient_wilderness: The wind carries whispers of danger.
I can track anything through this terrain.
idle_long: The prey escapes while we stand idle.
I did not leave my homeland to watch grass grow.
